dataset: CodiEsp v1.4 test set, officially distributed machine-translated English text_files_en
subset: first 50 sorted test article IDs; identical subset across DeepSeek comparisons

old_candidate_assisted.method: clinical text + TF-IDF/similar-case Top-20 candidate diagnosis codes -> DeepSeek selection

comparison.interpretation: Removing retrieval/candidate constraints and using an explicit exhaustive thinking prompt improved both precision and recall versus the old candidate-assisted DeepSeek configuration on the same 50 cases, but recall remains low. The dominant failure is under-coding: the direct model predicts 4.76 codes/case versus 11.42 gold codes/case on average.

scientific_boundary: This is a direct model-capability benchmark on CodiEsp clinical cases, not an RA-specific MIPA/MIMIC under-recording result. CodiEsp-D official leaderboard uses MAP; these exact-code micro precision/recall/F1 metrics are internal complementary diagnostics.
